LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED, ON DEFENDANT PENTA AVIATION SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO CONDUCT DISCOVERY DIRECTED TOWARDS GENERAL JURISDICTION IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED ANY LIKELIHOOD OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH JURISDICTION. EVEN IF DEFENDANT DID EXTENSIVE BUSINESS IN CANADA WITH A COMPANY BASED IN CALIFORNIA (AND THE EVIDENCE DOES NOT SUGGEST THE COMPANIES REFERRED TO BY PLAINTIFF ARE BASED IN CALIFORNIA), THAT WOULD NOT ESTABLISH GENERAL JURISDICTION IN CANADA. THE EVIDENCE BEFORE THE COURT ESTABLISHES THAT DEFENDANT LACKS ANY ON-GOING OR EXTENSIVE CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA THAT COULD SUPPORT THE EXERCISE OF JURISDICTION, AND THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE LAWSUIT HAS NO CONNECTION TO ANY ACTIVITY BY DEFENDANT IN CALIFORNIA. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar May-07-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Hession, Esq. of Clyde & Co, Tel. 415.365.9822, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was not contested, on DEFENDANT PENTA AVIATION SERVICES, INC.'s MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The motion to quash is granted. Plaintiff's request to conduct discovery directed towards general jurisdiction is denied because plaintiff has not demonstrated any likelihood of producing evidence to establish jurisdiction. Even if defendant did extensive business in Canada with a company based in California (and the evidence does not suggest the companies referred to by plaintiff are based in California), that would not establish general jurisdiction in Canada. The evidence before the Court establishes that defendant lacks any on-going or extensive contacts with California that could support the exercise of jurisdiction, and the subject matter of the lawsuit has no connection to any activity by defendant in California. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Apr-09-2013 Continued To May-07-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
